clues: Observed in the late 19th and early 20th century, this disease is always secondary to an underlying condition
Due to its high mortality, it has been grimly referred to as 'death is coming'
Also known as a consumption coagulopathy, early researchers identified this as a rapid widespread clot formation exhausting the body's clotting factors
The fourth leading cause of death worldwide, approximately 6% of the adult population in the US are affected
Recorded as far back as 1679, early descriptors used to describe the affected organs were 'voluminous' or 'turgid'
The Fletcher and Peto curve provided a foundational understanding of the natural history of this disease and the impact of smoking as well as smoking cessation
Do you know the original name for 7-Up? Discovered in 1817 in Sweden, this was an original ingredient in 7-Up until it was banned in the 1940s
A key component of red fireworks because of how it burns, modern medical uses of this were based on a misconception that this medication would treat excess uric acid in the brain
This famously can cause nephrogenic diabetes insipidus, affecting 10-40% of patients when used long term
Although you might think of other organs initially, this disease was first described by an otolaryngologist in 1897 who identifed rapid destruction of the nose and face
While lungs and kidneys are classic sites affected by small clusters of immune cells, almost any part of the body can be involved
In 2010, this disease was officially renamed due to historical ties of the prior eponymous discoverer with the Nazi movement
Documented for over 4,000 years, ancient practitioners viewed this as a spiritual curse
Historical treatments included remedies like covering the affected organ with warm bread or even surgeries drilling into nearby bone aimed to release 'trapped air'
Make sure to use proper ear protection. Loud noise exposure is a common cause
